lede: A look at August 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Mildred Maldonado and Brett Gardner, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Discharge of radioactive water of the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant
events.desc: Japan officially begins discharging treated radioactive water from the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant into the Pacific Ocean, sparking international concerns and condemnation.

events.title: International Astronomical Union
events.desc: The International Astronomical Union (IAU) redefines the term "planet" such that Pluto is now considered a dwarf planet.

events.title: Hurricane Andrew
events.desc: Hurricane Andrew makes landfall in Homestead, Florida as a Category 5 hurricane, causing up to $25 billion (1992 USD) in damages.

events.title: Buddhist crisis
events.desc: Buddhist crisis: As a result of the Xá Lợi Pagoda raids, the US State Department cables the United States Embassy, Saigon to encourage Army of the Republic of Vietnam generals to launch a coup against President Ngô Đình Diệm if he did not remove his brother Ngô Đình Nhu.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of the Eastern Solomons. Japanese aircraft carrier Ryūjō is sunk, with the loss of seven officers and 113 crewmen. The US carrier USS Enterprise is heavily damaged.

events.title: Sovereign Council of Asturias and León
events.desc: Spanish Civil War: Sovereign Council of Asturias and León is proclaimed in Gijón.

events.title: Battle of Cer
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Cer ends as the first Allied victory in the war.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: A small force of Pennsylvania militia is ambushed and overwhelmed by an American Indian group, which forces George Rogers Clark to abandon his attempt to attack Detroit.

events.title: Book of Common Prayer (1662)
events.desc: The 1662 Book of Common Prayer is legally enforced as the liturgy of the Church of England, precipitating the Great Ejection of Dissenter ministers from their benefices.

events.title: Dutch expedition to Valdivia
events.desc: A Dutch fleet establishes a new colony in the ruins of Valdivia in southern Chile.

births.name: Stephen Fry
births.line: English actor, journalist, producer, and screenwriter

births.name: Marsha P. Johnson
births.line: American gay liberation activist and drag queen (died 1992)

deaths.text: Mike Flanagan (baseball), American baseball player, coach, and sportscaster
Malcolm Kirk, English rugby player and wrestler
Getúlio Vargas, Brazilian lawyer and politician, 14th President of Brazil

observances.text: Christian feast day: Bartholomew the Apostle (Roman Catholic, Anglican)
Christian feast day: Massa Candida (Martyrs of Utica)
